page: Microsoft Azure D128ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but supports Premium Storage for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier multi-threaded performance, highlighted by a multi-core Geekbench score of 21581.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 107868.96. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), drive exceptional throughput in specialized workloads, such as software compilation and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 516.29 GB/sec for large-block read operations. However, single-core performance is comparatively moderate, as shown by average ratings in stress-ng single-core tests.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128ls_v6 maximizes compute density by pairing a high-frequency, 128-vCPU processor with a streamlined 256.0 GB memory footprint. This resource allocation is highly cost-effective for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory arrays. The server is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference, but is less optimal for memory-bound applications or workloads requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D128ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its high Passmark CPU score of 107868.96 and exceptional memory read bandwidth of 516.29 GB/sec. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. The hardware profile is optimized for compute-dense workloads, offering excellent qualitative cost efficiency for tasks that prioritize raw processing power over high memory capacity. It is highly suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128ls_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, 128-vCPU compute-optimized virtual machine designed for intensive multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and database operations.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
256.0 GB of system memory providing a streamlined 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
No local storage or GPUs, utilizing remote Premium Storage instead
Best suited for compute-dense workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded decompression
